meaning: n.[生]有生源说,胚种论(亦作 panspermatism,panspermy)
examples: If this were true, it would also give excellent evidence to support the theory of "panspermia."
That raises the possibility that life on Earth arrived here on a meteorite - a concept called panspermia.
The professor and his late colleague Sir Fred Hoyle championed the so-calledd " panspermia" theory from the 1960s.
For more than a century, panspermia has fallen into the large category of scientific ideas that capture the imagination but are completely unverifiable.
An even more intriguing question and the one that the original theory of panspermia tried to address is whether life might have arrived on earth from another solar system.
